title: OpenAI: GenAI Inference API (GPT-4, DALL-E)
description: Industry-leading AI models (GPT-4, ChatGPT, DALL-E) via OpenAI API; production-grade reliability; premium pricing; best-in-class text and image generation.

cost.costNotes: Premium pricing reflects best-in-class quality. Implement token counting, caching, and usage monitoring to control costs. Consider GPT-3.5-turbo for cost-sensitive use cases.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Pay-as-you-go token-based pricing creates highly variable costs. Min ($20): Small-scale usage (~10K tokens/day with GPT-3.5-turbo at $0.002/1K tokens = ~$0.60/day). Max ($10,000): Heavy production usage (~1M tokens/day with GPT-4 at $0.03-0.06/1K tokens = $30-60K tokens cost, or high-volume DALL-E image generation at $0.04-0.12/image for ~3K-8K images/day). Typical production chatbot handling 100K tokens/day: $100-300/month. Enterprise applications with millions of tokens can easily exceed $10K/month.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low-to-medium complexity integration. Simple REST API with excellent documentation and official SDKs (Python, Node.js, etc.) enables rapid implementation. Basic integration: 2-4 hours ($200-400 at $100/hr dev rate). Production-ready implementation requires: error handling, rate limiting, token counting/monitoring, prompt engineering, response validation, and cost controls (additional 4-8 hours, $400-800). Total: 6-12 hours. Senior developers can integrate in <1 day, but proper production hardening, testing, and optimization add complexity. No specialized ML expertise required, but understanding token economics and prompt optimization is essential.
provenance.licenseCost: Pure consumption-based pricing model with no upfront license fees or subscriptions. Costs are entirely usage-based (pay-per-token for text models, pay-per-image for DALL-E). New accounts receive $5-18 in free trial credits for testing, but no ongoing free tier for production use. Enterprise customers may negotiate volume discounts or committed use agreements, but standard access requires no license purchase - just API key registration and pay-as-you-go billing.

provides.reasoning: Core capability - this pattern provides generative AI inference services via OpenAI's API including GPT-4, ChatGPT, and DALL-E models.
Specific capability provided by GPT-4 models for generating text content across various use cases and formats.
Conversational AI capability provided by ChatGPT models for interactive dialogue and multi-turn conversations.
DALL-E provides image generation from text prompts, a distinct capability from text generation for visual content creation.
OpenAI API provides text embedding models for semantic search, similarity tasks, and vector representations of text.
GPT-4 supports structured function calling which enables the model to output structured data and trigger external functions.
OpenAI offers content moderation API as part of their service to detect potentially harmful or unsafe content.
OpenAI text-embedding models (text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-3-large) provide embedding generation from raw text, converting inputs into dense vector representations for downstream use.

requires.reasoning: HTTP/REST client needed to communicate with OpenAI's API endpoints for all model interactions.
API keys must be securely stored and retrieved to authenticate with OpenAI services and prevent unauthorized access.
Token tracking is essential for cost management and staying within model context limits as GPT-4 has specific token constraints.
Premium pricing model requires tracking API usage costs to prevent budget overruns and optimize spending.
OpenAI API has rate limits that need to be handled to prevent throttling and ensure smooth operation.
API calls may fail due to various reasons and need proper retry strategies and error recovery mechanisms.

availability.reason: Pattern for public SaaS with widespread adoption; documentation freely available.

supports_nfr.reason: OpenAI's managed API service provides up to 99.9% availability SLA for production workloads.
OpenAI API typical p95 latency for GPT-4 inference is 1-5 seconds due to model complexity and processing overhead.
OpenAI API p99 latency for complex GPT-4 requests typically ranges 2-10 seconds depending on token count and model load.
OpenAI's global load balancing and automatic failover enables recovery within 1-5 minutes for API service disruptions.
OpenAI API enterprise tier supports up to 1000 requests/second (3.6M per hour) with rate limit quotas.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: OpenAI API is cloud-based SaaS accessible from any cloud provider or on-premises environments via HTTP.
OpenAI provides official SDKs for Python and TypeScript with community libraries available for all major languages.
OpenAI API is HTTP-based and callable from any platform that can make REST requests over HTTPS.
Pattern specifically requires OpenAI as the backend provider
Pattern is specifically designed for AI/ML inference using OpenAI models and requires this capability.

primary_model.description: Primary text generation model. gpt-4o: best performance/cost balance; gpt-4-turbo: faster with vision; gpt-4: highest quality; gpt-3.5-turbo: fastest/cheapest; o1: advanced reasoning; o1-mini: cost-effective reasoning.

fallback_model.description: Fallback model if primary fails or rate-limited. gpt-4o-mini: balanced fallback; gpt-3.5-turbo: fastest fallback; none: fail without fallback.

image_model.description: Image generation model. dall-e-3: highest quality, more expensive; dall-e-2: faster, cheaper; none: disable image generation.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens per response. Higher values allow longer responses but increase cost and latency. Model-dependent limits apply.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0-2). Lower values (0-0.3): deterministic, factual; Medium (0.7-1.0): balanced creativity; Higher (1.5-2.0): maximum creativity and randomness.

enable_streaming.description: Stream responses token-by-token for better UX in interactive applications. Disable for batch processing or when full response needed before processing.

timeout_seconds.description: API request timeout in seconds. Longer timeouts for complex prompts or image generation; shorter for interactive applications.

retry_strategy.description: Retry strategy for rate limits and transient failures. exponential-backoff: recommended for production; fixed-delay: simpler but less efficient; none: fail immediately.

service_auth_method.description: Service credential method used to authenticate to the LLM provider API. Always api_key — stored in environment variable, never in code.

warn_nfr.reason: OpenAI API has premium pricing (~$0.03-0.12 per 1K tokens). At <10 jobs/hour, consider cheaper alternatives or local models.
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--openai: peak_jobs_per_hour is {actual} (<10). Premium OpenAI pricing may not be cost-effective at low volumes; consider cheaper alternatives.

warn_nfr.reason: OpenAI processes data on external servers. Sending PII to third-party APIs may violate compliance requirements (GDPR).
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--openai: spec indicates PII handling, but OpenAI API processes data externally which may violate data residency or compliance requirements.
